operation: First, a custom reference plane is created by rotating the standard XZ workplane 10 degrees around the Y-axis. Next, four 2D control points (1,1), (-1,1), (-1,-1), (1,-1) are defined on the tilted plane. A closed spline wire is generated by approximating a path through these four points with a tolerance of 0.001, then the closed spline wire is used to construct a single planar face. Finally, the resulting face is exported as an STL mesh file.
geometry: The model is a single zero-thickness planar face tilted 10 degrees away from the XZ plane around the Y axis. The face has a smooth, closed, symmetric rounded-square perimeter formed by a spline that passes through four symmetric corner points positioned 1 unit from the plane origin along both axes of the tilted host plane. The entire structure is a flat 2D surface with even, curved edges and two axes of symmetry aligned to the tilted plane's local axes.
function: This is a generic symmetric curved-quadrilateral planar surface template, designed to serve as a base profile, patch surface, or starting geometry for subsequent 3D CAD operations such as extrusion, cutting, or lofting in custom part design workflows.
